title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
summary: David Ondrej discusses the banning of Fable 5, framing it as marking the beginning of a permanent underclass. The video appears to connect product restrictions to broader concerns about AI access and opportunity.
writeup: David Ondrej examines the banning of Fable 5, treating the restriction as a signal of a permanent underclass forming around AI access. The argument links a single product ban to a wider adoption gap, where limits on tools translate into unequal opportunity. The takeaway: product restrictions are framed not as isolated decisions but as markers of who gets to participate in AI and who is locked out.
